in the interview for becoming eligible for selection and hence, 
his candidature cannot be considered for selection although he 
may have secured more marks than the selected candidates in 
the main examinations. The learned counsel also submitted that 
1 candidate with locomotor disability and 1 candidate with low 
vision disability, i.e., total 2 candidates with disabilities in the 
unreserved category secured more than the minimum marks in 
the interview and were duly selected in the 2021 examination. 
Hence, the reliance placed by the appellant on Section 34 
(2) of the RPwD Act, 2016 is also misplaced, inasmuch as 
mere availability of the appellant was not sufficient, and he 
was not eligible for selection, since he had not secured the 
minimum marks in the interview. Since other candidates with 
benchmark disabilities (including a candidate with low vision) 
were duly selected in the selection process, the appellant
